component.name: Foresight
component.summary: Preview and run rsync transfers with a dry-run change list

description.p: Foresight is a GNOME-native front-end for rsync. Every sync starts as a preview: a real dry run, rendered as a grouped list of changes — created, updated, deleted, and attributes-only — so a mistake is obvious before a single byte moves.

ul.li: Grouped change preview from a real rsync dry run before every transfer
Add files and folders from different locations as sources, or drop them in
Live transfer view with a progress bar and a structured activity log
Mirror deletions (--delete) gated behind a confirmation of exactly what would be removed
Advanced options: move, unit-aware bandwidth limit, excludes, and free-form extra arguments
Reusable presets for your Advanced-option sets
An honest, in-app inventory of exactly which rsync flags this build exposes

description.p: The rsync engine is bundled and version-pinned, and all file access flows through desktop portals — Foresight never asks for your whole home directory.

screenshot.caption: Configure a job: add sources and a destination
Preview every change, grouped, before it runs
Advanced options: move, bandwidth limit, excludes, and extra flags
An honest, in-app list of what Foresight can do

description.p: First release. Multi-source jobs, grouped dry-run preview, live transfer with a structured log, mirror-deletion confirmation, advanced options with presets, and an in-app capability inventory.
